title: Macroeconomic and Foreign Exchange Policies of Major Trading Partners of the United States
summary: This page is a Treasury Department archive index listing historical reports on international macroeconomic and exchange rate policies from 1989 through January 2026. No actual report content, analysis, or policy determinations are present; only navigation links to past reports are provided.
key_points: This is a listing/navigation page, not an actual report or analysis document.
References a January 2026 Report and numerous past reports dating back to 1989.
The page serves as an index to the semiannual 'Report to Congress on International Economic and Exchange Rate Policies'.
No currency manipulation determinations, exchange rate assessments, or policy conclusions are present in this source.
Actual report content (e.g., China FX policy analysis, Treasury's official determinations) would be in separate report documents.
why_it_matters: This page does not contain archiveable research content. It is merely a directory to historical Treasury reports. The actual semiannual reports contain policy-relevant analysis on major trading partners' exchange rate practices, which could trigger FX market reactions depending on Treasury's currency manipulation designations. Users seeking macro catalysts information should access the individual report documents (e.g., June 2025 Report, January 2026 Report) rather than this navigation index.

issues_found: Source is a listing/navigation page, not an article or report - violates Core Rule 5
event_type is 'unknown' but should not be - the page itself is not a report and cannot be assigned a meaningful event_type from the taxonomy

notes: Non-archiveable: This is a Treasury archive index/listing page, not a report itself. Contains only navigation links to historical reports. The semiannual 'Report to Congress on International Economic and Exchange Rate Policies' is the substantive document series; this page merely indexes past reports from 1989-2026. To obtain actual macro catalyst content (e.g., Treasury's currency manipulation determinations, FX policy assessments), the individual report URLs must be accessed separately.
